symbols.name: new
symbols.summary: Creates a new `PangoOTRuleset` for the given OpenType info.

symbols.summary: Creates a new `PangoOTBuffer` for the given OpenType font.

symbols.name: TableType
symbols.summary: The PangoOTTableType enumeration values are used to identify the various OpenType tables in the pango_ot_info_… functions.

symbols.summary: Finds the OpenType language-system tag best describing @language.

symbols.name: tag_to_language
symbols.summary: Finds a `PangoLanguage` corresponding to @language_tag.

symbols.name: TAG_MAKE
symbols.summary: Creates a `PangoOTTag` from four characters.  This is similar and compatible with the FT_MAKE_TAG() macro from FreeType.

symbols.name: add_feature
symbols.summary: Adds a feature to the ruleset.

symbols.name: add_glyph
symbols.summary: Appends a glyph to a `PangoOTBuffer`, with @properties identifying which features should be applied on this glyph.  See [method@PangoOT.Ruleset.add_feature].

symbols.summary: Empties a `PangoOTBuffer`, make it ready to add glyphs to.

symbols.summary: Destroys a `PangoOTBuffer` and free all associated memory.

symbols.summary: Gets the number of GSUB and GPOS features in the ruleset.

symbols.summary: Computes a hash of a `PangoOTRulesetDescription` structure suitable to be used, for example, as an argument to g_hash_table_new().

symbols.name: list_features
symbols.summary: Obtains the list of features for the given language of the given script.

symbols.name: list_languages
symbols.summary: Obtains the list of available languages for a given script.

symbols.name: list_scripts
symbols.summary: Obtains the list of available scripts.

symbols.name: position
symbols.summary: Performs the OpenType GPOS positioning on @buffer using the features in @ruleset.

symbols.name: set_rtl
symbols.summary: Sets whether glyphs will be rendered right-to-left.  This setting is needed for proper horizontal positioning of right-to-left scripts.

symbols.name: substitute
symbols.summary: Performs the OpenType GSUB substitution on @buffer using the features in @ruleset.
